Measure the degree and robustness of norms the model acquires distributionally from the corpus, and attribute them to the legal and ethical dialogues. Deferred.

An experiment that quantifies intuitive alignment: how strongly and how robustly the model holds normative judgments that were never stated as rules but emerged from the distribution of the training data, especially the reasoning dialogues. Because the corpus is fully controlled and the model starts value-free, the measured alignment can be attributed to specific injected signal.

This experiment treats alignment as a measurable, data-driven property rather than a bolted-on refusal policy. The model has no prior values, so any normative lean is a statistical product of the corpus and can be traced to it. The design has three parts. Dose-response: inject a norm at varying density and consistency, then measure generalization to held-out situations across near transfer, far transfer, and adversarial reframings. Reasoned versus bare: deliver the same norm as argued legal or ethical dialogue versus flat verdicts, and compare robustness, testing the hypothesis that norms carrying their reasons generalize more robustly because the model learns the generative principle rather than the label. Scoring: define an intuitive-alignment score as the consistency of normative judgments across a probe set, with robustness measured as how much each judgment survives paraphrase, reframing, and compositional novelty. The expected contribution is attribution, since controlled data makes it possible to connect measured alignment to specific dialogues, which is impractical in models whose pretraining already carried values. Distributional alignment is entangled with the world model and so may generalize better than surface refusal, but it is a propensity rather than a hard floor and remains defeasible by the same reasoning that makes the model capable. The experiment depends on the reasoning dialogues as the normative signal and on the evaluation machinery as the measurement vehicle, and is deferred until those are built.
